Requirement_Summary: Define, approve, and annually review an enterprise cybersecurity policy covering scope, roles, risk appetite, and control baselines.
Evidence_Examples: Approved policy, review minutes, distribution records

Requirement_Summary: Assign accountable owners for cybersecurity, define RACI, and ensure separation of duties for administration and audit.
Evidence_Examples: Org charts, role descriptions, access approvals

Requirement_Summary: Adopt recognized security controls framework and tailor to organizational risk (e.g., benchmark or regulatory mapping).
Perform periodic risk assessments, document risks, treatment plans, and track residual risk.
Identify applicable laws/regulations and monitor compliance posture and remediation.
Formal process to record, approve, time-bound, and monitor security exceptions.
Assess and monitor third-party cybersecurity, include security clauses and right-to-audit.
Define BIA, RTO/RPO targets, recovery strategies, and test plans annually.
Implement DR for critical systems, perform failover tests, and validate data integrity.
Define incident classifications, runbooks, communication, and escalation procedures; test semi-annually.
Preserve logs and chain-of-custody for investigations; ensure legal admissibility.
Enforce MFA for privileged and user access; disable legacy protocols where possible.
Azure_Policy_Name: Users must authenticate with multi-factor authentication to create or update resources
Defender_Recommendation: Enable MFA - Require multifactor authentication for all users

Requirement_Summary: Use role-based access, just-in-time elevation, and periodic access reviews.
Defender_Recommendation: Manage access and permissions - Use role-based access control

Requirement_Summary: Manage keys/secrets in hardened vaults, rotate, and enforce CMK for sensitive workloads.
Azure_Policy_Name: OS and data disks should be encrypted with a customer-managed key
Defender_Recommendation: Enable encryption at rest with customer-managed keys

Requirement_Summary: Eliminate local/shared keys and legacy admin accounts; require identity-based auth.
Segment networks, isolate sensitive workloads, enforce east-west controls.
Azure_Policy_Name: Subnets should be associated with a Network Security Group

Requirement_Summary: Disable public endpoints and require Private Link/private endpoints for data services.
Azure_Policy_Name: App Configuration should use private link
Defender_Recommendation: Secure cloud services with network controls - Use Private Link

Requirement_Summary: Use Azure Firewall/WAF, DDoS Standard, and secure ingress/egress.
Require TLS 1.2+, enforce HTTPS-only, and disable insecure ciphers.
Defender_Recommendation: Encrypt data in transit - Enforce TLS 1.2+ encryption

Requirement_Summary: Classify data, apply labels, and enforce protection policies by sensitivity.
Encrypt data at rest with CMK where required; enforce TLS in transit.
Azure_Policy_Name: PostgreSQL servers should use customer-managed keys to encrypt data at rest
Defender_Recommendation: Enable encryption at rest and in transit

Requirement_Summary: Apply DLP across channels; monitor and block sensitive data exfiltration.
Back up critical data, validate restorability, and protect backup immutability.
Enable diagnostic logs and forward to a central SIEM/Log Analytics.
Azure_Policy_Name: Enable logging by category group for Log Analytics workspaces to Log Analytics

Requirement_Summary: Enable cloud workload posture monitoring and real-time alerts.
Defender_Recommendation: Enable enhanced security features - Microsoft Defender for Cloud

Requirement_Summary: Define log retention, audit coverage, and tamper-evident storage.
Defender_Recommendation: Enable auditing and logging with retention policies

Requirement_Summary: Continuously assess and patch VMs, containers, databases, and apps.
Azure_Policy_Name: System updates should be installed on your machines (powered by Azure Update Manager)
Defender_Recommendation: Apply system updates and remediate vulnerabilities

Requirement_Summary: Threat modeling, code scanning, dependency management (SBOM), and secure builds.
Formal change approval, peer review, and rollback strategies.
Assess model risks, document intended use, and test for harmful behavior.
Defender_Recommendation: Implement AI governance and risk management

Requirement_Summary: Control prompts/responses, apply DLP, and safeguard training/grounding data.
Defender_Recommendation: Implement data governance for AI workloads

Requirement_Summary: Place AI services in private networks, restrict outbound, and protect secrets.
Azure_Policy_Name: Cognitive Services should use private link
Defender_Recommendation: Secure AI services with private endpoints

Requirement_Summary: Require Customer Lockbox and no data persistence for sensitive AI workloads.
Ensure DLP and sensitivity labels protect data used by Copilot; restrict summarization of highly confidential items.
Defender_Recommendation: Implement sensitivity labels and DLP for Copilot

Requirement_Summary: Enable auditing and retention for Copilot interactions and outputs as applicable.
Include AI-generated content and interactions in eDiscovery and legal hold.
Defender_Recommendation: Implement eDiscovery and legal hold policies

Requirement_Summary: Monitor Copilot usage for inappropriate or noncompliant content.
